text: In the field of digital media steganography, the dominant paradigm involves embedding payloads by minimizing a carefully defined distortion function. Because modern coding techniques can already approach the theoretical payload-distortion bound, the central challenge for researchers is to design distortion functions that offer high resistance to empirical statistical detection. In this paper, we introduce Universal Wavelet Relative Distortion (UNIWARD), a versatile framework for distortion design that is applicable across diverse embedding domains. This approach computes distortion as the sum of relative changes across coefficients in a directional filter bank decomposition of the cover image. By leveraging directionality, UNIWARD concentrates embedding modifications in regions that are inherently difficult to model, such as textures and noisy areas, while successfully avoiding smooth regions and clean edges. Experimental evaluations using both high-dimensional rich models and targeted attacks demonstrate that UNIWARD-based methods match or exceed the performance of current state-of-the-art schemes in the spatial, JPEG, and side-informed JPEG domains.
